title: Disassembling Imported High-End Wardrobes: Keeping Every Cam-Lock and Pin Intact During a Multi-District Transition
excerpt: Disassemble imported high-end wardrobes: cam-lock pin intact multi-district move, European closet systems packing relocation guide.
content:
Disassembling imported high-end wardrobes: keeping every cam-lock and pin intact during a multi-district transition

Imported European wardrobes use cam-locks, dowels, and numbered panels—force disassembly loses pin, strips thread, voids warranty. Multi-district transition demands photo sequence, labeled bags per door, and reassembly manual bag taped inside panel one.

This guide disassembles imported high-end wardrobes keeping every cam-lock and pin intact.

Disassembly sequence

  • Photograph fully assembled wide and close-ups hardware.
  • Remove doors first label D1 D2.
  • Drawers out pack contents separate.
  • Cam-lock key or hex turn release track.
  • Bag hardware per section zip labeled.
  • Tape manual and Allen key panel interior.

Protection transit

Edge cardboard panels; blanket wrap; vertical store doors; no stack crush; climate chipboard swell Kremt—sealed wrap.

Multi-district logistics

Origin narrow stair may require full knockdown; destination elevator measure; same crew reassemble preferred continuity.

Reassembly destination

Level floor check; don't overtighten cam; adjust hinges last; missing pin call manufacturer part before hack.

Active Movers wardrobe tier

Trained knockdown crew; hardware bag system; reassembly optional line item.

Conclusion

Imported wardrobe survives multi-district move when cam-locks bagged and photos sequenced—high-end closet reassembles factory tight not wobble mystery.
author: Active Movers Team
